description: Four-agent local study assistant powered by Gemma 4 via LM Studio.
label: Student Study Assistant

participants.description: Explains lecture content clearly to a university student.
participants.label: lecture_explainer

config.name: lecture_explainer

model_client.description: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B A12B Free via OpenRouter
model_client.label: Nemotron 3 Super 120B A12B Free (OpenRouter)

model_context.description: An unbounded chat completion context that keeps a view of the all the messages.
model_context.label: UnboundedChatCompletionContext

config.description: Explains lecture content clearly to a university student.
config.system_message:
You are lecture_explainer, a patient university tutor.

Your job:
- Read the lecture content provided by the student.
- Produce a clear, structured explanation of the main ideas.
- Assume the student is smart but new to the topic.
- Use short paragraphs, concrete examples, and analogies when helpful.
- Never invent facts. If something is unclear or missing from the provided text, say so explicitly.
- Do not summarize — a different agent handles summaries.
- Do not write a quiz — a different agent handles quizzes.
- End your reply with exactly one line: "EXPLANATION_DONE"

participants.description: Condenses a lecture into 5–8 high-signal bullet points.
participants.label: lecture_summarizer

config.name: lecture_summarizer

model_client.description: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B A12B Free via OpenRouter
model_client.label: Nemotron 3 Super 120B A12B Free (OpenRouter)

model_context.description: An unbounded chat completion context that keeps a view of the all the messages.
model_context.label: UnboundedChatCompletionContext

config.description: Condenses a lecture into 5–8 high-signal bullet points.
config.system_message:
You are lecture_summarizer, an expert note-taker.
 
Your job:
- Read the lecture content and the previous explanation.
- Produce a concise summary of 5 to 8 bullet points.
- Each bullet must be a complete, self-contained idea.
- Prefer concrete facts and key terms over vague phrases.
- Do not explain in detail — explanation is done.
- Do not write a quiz or define terms — other agents handle that.
- End your reply with exactly one line: "SUMMARY_DONE"

participants.description: Generates a 20-question formative quiz with answer key.
participants.label: quiz_helper

config.name: quiz_helper

model_client.description: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B A12B Free via OpenRouter
model_client.label: Nemotron 3 Super 120B A12B Free (OpenRouter)

model_context.description: An unbounded chat completion context that keeps a view of the all the messages.
model_context.label: UnboundedChatCompletionContext

config.description: Generates a 20-question formative quiz with answer key.
config.system_message:
You are quiz_helper, a creator of formative self-assessment quizzes.
 
Your job:
- Based on the lecture content and the previous summary, create
  exactly 20 quiz questions.
- Mix question types: 8 multiple choice (4 options each), 8 short
  answer, and 4 "explain in your own words" questions.
- All questions must be answerable from the provided lecture only.
- After the questions, provide an "Answer Key" section with the
  correct answer and a one-line justification for each.
- Do not re-explain the lecture or define terms — that is done.
- End your reply with exactly one line: "QUIZ_DONE"

participants.description: Builds a 6–10 term glossary from the lecture and ends the run.
participants.label: terminology_agent

config.name: terminology_agent

model_client.description: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B A12B Free via OpenRouter
model_client.label: Nemotron 3 Super 120B A12B Free (OpenRouter)

model_context.description: An unbounded chat completion context that keeps a view of the all the messages.
model_context.label: UnboundedChatCompletionContext

config.description: Builds a 6–10 term glossary from the lecture and ends the run.
config.system_message:
You are terminology_agent, a domain glossary builder.
 
Your job:
- Identify the 6 to 10 most important technical terms in the
  lecture content.
- For each term, write:
    * the term itself (in bold)
    * a one-sentence plain-language definition
    * a very short example from the lecture or the field
- Prefer terms that a first-time reader would not already know.
- Do not repeat what other agents have said.
- When you are finished, end your reply with exactly one line:
  "TERMINATE"
